concerns.text: Task T031 implements 'Conditional Bootstrap Logic' that skips execution if N < 3. This silently weakens FR-006 which mandates 'System MUST execute bootstrap resampling (1000 iterations)'. The plan explicitly allows a 'Deterministic Mode' exception for N < 3, but the task list fails to capture this as a conditional scope change, effectively dropping the requirement to handle the N < 3 case as a valid, documented state rather than a silent skip.

Task T036 validates constraints on a 'subset (1 snapshot, 2 resolutions)'. FR-007 and SC-004 define the success criterion against a specific load profile: '5 cases of 1024³ grids with 4 resolution levels'. Testing on a subset is a semantic weakening of the acceptance criterion; it does not verify the system meets the 6-hour runtime or 7GB memory limit under the defined stress load, leaving the SC-004 verification incomplete.

Task T035 logs runtime but lacks a verification step to compare the logged value against the 6-hour threshold defined in FR-007 and SC-004. Without an explicit task to assert 'runtime <= 6h' and fail the build if exceeded, the success criterion is not actively enforced, constituting a dropped acceptance criterion.

Task T016 adds memory assertions in code, but SC-005 requires measuring peak memory usage against the 7GB limit. There is no dedicated task to execute a full pipeline stress test and explicitly log/verify the peak RSS value against the threshold before proceeding, leaving the SC-005 verification passive rather than active.
